title: 83(b) Election Letter
description: The standard IRS filing letter to report income on unvested restricted stock grants in the tax year of the grant.
whatsCovered: Taxpayer identification and Social Security details
Description of the restricted property / share grant
Fair Market Value of shares on grant date
IRS filing instructions and certified mail guidelines
whenToUse: Use this IRS template letter within 30 days of receiving a restricted stock grant (e.g. during founder incorporation) to optimize your taxes.

fields.label: Company Office Address
fields.placeholder: e.g. 100 Innovators Blvd, San Francisco, CA

clauses.heading: 83(B) ELECTION LETTER
clauses.body: This startup agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of {effective_date} (the "Effective Date"), by and between {company_name}, located at {company_address} (the "Company"), and the counterparty or stakeholders executing this document below.

clauses.heading: 1. Core Agreement Terms
clauses.body: This agreement sets out the transaction and operational terms for 83(b) Election Letter. The parties agree to coordinate in good faith to complete milestones, share resources, or grant rights as specified.

clauses.heading: 2. IRS Section 83(b) Declaration
clauses.body: Taxpayer {taxpayer_name} elects to report the restricted grant of {shares_acquired} shares carrying a fair market value of {fair_market_value} in the current year.

clauses.heading: 3. Proprietary Information and Secrecy
clauses.body: Both parties agree to treat all financial metrics, corporate plans, cap tables, and research files received during the execution of this Agreement as confidential and proprietary.

clauses.heading: 4. Governing Law
clauses.body: This Agreement shall be governed and interpreted under the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}.

faq.question: What is the 83(b) filing deadline?
faq.answer: You must file the letter with the IRS within exactly 30 days of the stock grant date. There are no extensions or exceptions.

faq.question: Why is an 83(b) election beneficial?
faq.answer: It allows you to pay taxes on the value of the shares at the time of grant (low value) rather than paying taxes as they vest (high value).
